# Quote

An inline quotation component that renders text with quotation marks. Renders a `<q>` HTML element, which is the semantic element for short inline quotations.

## Import

```jsx
import { Quote } from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
```

## Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` | - | The quoted text content |
| `cite` | `string` | - | URL of the quote source |
| `ref` | `React.Ref` | - | Forwarded ref to the underlying element |

All standard Chakra UI style props are also supported.

## Usage

### Basic

```jsx
<Text>
  As the saying goes, <Quote>actions speak louder than words</Quote>.
</Text>
```

### With citation

```jsx
<Text>
  The documentation states <Quote cite="https://example.com/docs">all props are optional</Quote>.
</Text>
```

### With styling

```jsx
<Quote color="gray.600" fontStyle="italic">
  To be or not to be
</Quote>
```

## Features

- Renders a semantic `<q>` HTML element for inline quotations
- Automatically wraps content in quotation marks (browser default behavior)
- Supports the `cite` attribute for referencing the source URL
- Supports all Chakra UI style props for custom styling
- Forwards refs to the underlying DOM element
- Lightweight wrapper with no additional overhead
